steps.title: Attach the opening modifier
steps.content: The opening phrase "A pioneer in the field of taphonomy" must describe the noun that immediately follows it. The sentence therefore needs Anna Behrensmeyer to appear right after that phrase.

steps.title: Reject dangling subjects
steps.content: A would make fossils the pioneer. C would make "it" the pioneer. D would make a 2005 analysis the pioneer. All three create illogical modifier placement.

steps.title: Use the clear subject
steps.content: Putting Anna Behrensmeyer after the modifier and making her the subject of "demonstrated" creates a grammatical sentence. The answer is B.
